what_it_does: Renders the Templates canvas: a gallery of saved Build/Workshop runs treated as reusable parameterised templates. Decorates each saved template with its parent Type from CV_REGISTRY, groups them by parent Type (or shows a flat grid), and for each renders a TemplateCard with editable parameter/variable inputs and a Run button. resolveBrief substitutes {{key}} placeholders in a brief pattern with the entered values. Exposes Templates on window.
observations: Top-level destructures useState/useMemo/useEffect from React into aliased names (useState_tp etc.) to avoid bundle-scope collisions.
Subscribes to CV_REGISTRY changes via subscribe() and bumps a tick to re-decorate when the registry updates.
Maps each saved template to a parent Type id of form `doc.${t.doc.type}` and resolves it from CV_REGISTRY.
Two layouts: 'flat' (one grid) and default 'grouped' (sections per parent Type with a TypeLayerBadge and description).
Empty state instructs the user to save from Build or Workshop and explains Templates are children of Types.
TemplateCard distinguishes two kinds: workshop-doc (uses t.variables, materialized via WS_AI.materializeTemplate) vs brief templates (uses t.params + briefPattern resolved by resolveBrief).
Builds a 'fauxType' merging parent Type defaults with the template's doc to drive a TypeThumb preview.
isDirty compares current values against defaults to label the run button 'Run with edits'.
Delete uses window.confirm; failures route to window.dsaToast.
Heavy use of inline style objects referencing CSS custom properties (var(--font-display), var(--accent-gold-faint), etc.) rather than classes.
standouts: Templates are explicitly modeled as instances/children of registry Types — every filled parameter is framed as a registered Variable on the parent.
resolveBrief leaves unresolved {{key}} placeholders intact (returns the literal placeholder if no value) rather than blanking them.
fauxType construction lets a saved template render a live thumbnail through the same TypeThumb engine used for real Types.
conventions: Follows the project's registry-projection principle: the UI reads template grouping and Types from CV_REGISTRY rather than a parallel list.
Uses window-global components (CanvasHeader, TypeLayerBadge, TypeThumb, ViShape) and exposes Templates on window — consistent with the bundle's no-import pattern.
Deviates from the token/class discipline by embedding many inline style objects (though they reference design tokens via var()).
Aliased React hook destructuring to keep top-level names unique across the shared bundle.
concerns: Large volume of inline style objects duplicates styling that could live in CSS classes — values are tokenised but the layout/typography is hand-set per element.

Relies on several optional globals (WS_AI, TypeThumb, TypeLayerBadge, CV_REGISTRY) with optional chaining; if absent, cards silently render without thumbnails or fail to materialize with only a toast.
useEffect subscribe returns the subscribe() result directly as cleanup — correctness depends on CV_REGISTRY.subscribe returning an unsubscribe function.
notes: The Templates gallery: saved runs become re-runnable parameterised templates, grouped under their parent registry Type, each card letting you edit variables and re-run to fork a new variant.
questions: Does CV_REGISTRY.subscribe return an unsubscribe function (used as the effect cleanup)?
What is the shape of a saved template object (params vs variables vs briefPattern vs doc), and who writes them?
Where is WS_AI.materializeTemplate defined and what does it return on success?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header comment (saved Build runs as reusable parameterised templates, grouped by parent Type, with a Types mode) describes exactly what the component implements.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth: templates derive structure and grouping from the Type Registry.
Parameterised reuse: every saved run becomes a template forkable by editing variables.
Types→Templates lineage (template is a child of a doc Type).
UI as a projection of the registry.
intention_signals: Aims to turn ad-hoc generative runs into a structured, reusable library where each template is a registered child of a Type, editable by its declared variables, so re-running with new values composes fresh variants while preserving lineage to the parent Type.
novelty: The fauxType merge that gives a saved template a live registry-style thumbnail, and the explicit framing of saved runs as parameterised Type-children with editable Variables.
contribution: Contributes the Templates canvas UI and its model: grouping saved runs by parent Type, the TemplateCard with per-variable inputs and dirty-state run labeling, the workshop-doc vs brief-template branching, and the resolveBrief {{key}} substitution helper.
summary_for_embedding: A React canvas that lists saved Build/Workshop runs as reusable parameterised templates, grouped by their parent registry Type, with editable-variable cards, live thumbnails, and a brief-placeholder resolver.
intention_for_embedding: To make generative runs reusable as Type-anchored templates whose declared variables can be re-edited and re-run to compose fresh variants while keeping lineage to the parent Type.
